commands.review_summary:
The branch adds a shared user-facing assistant-error formatter, routes embedded lifecycle and reply payload error text through it, and adds regression tests for raw error canary suppression.

PR surface: Source +33, Tests +45. Total +78 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main still routes unclassified formatter output into lifecycle and reply payload text, while the formatter's final fallback returns raw error text. I did not run a live channel repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The remaining blocker is maintainer judgment on user-facing diagnostic compatibility and optional live channel proof, not a narrow automation repair.

target.title: fix: suppress raw provider errors in channel delivery
target.body:
Fixes #69737.

## Summary

This change blocks raw unclassified assistant `errorMessage` text at the user-facing delivery boundary.

It adds a shared `formatUserFacingAssistantErrorText(...)` wrapper around the existing assistant error formatter. Classified friendly errors still use the existing sanitizer/classification behavior, but formatter output that is only raw upstream pass-through now falls back to:

```text
LLM request failed.
```

Both terminal lifecycle events and reply payload construction now use that shared user-facing wrapper. Raw diagnostic details are not globally deleted, provider-side error objects are not changed, and internal observation fields such as `rawErrorPreview` remain available for maintainers.

## Evidence

- Before patch, lifecycle used:

```ts
const errorText = (friendlyError || lastAssistant.errorMessage || "LLM request failed.").trim();
```

- Before patch, reply payloads used:

```ts
formatAssistantErrorText(assistantForPayload, ...)
```

and pushed that text as an `isError` reply payload.

- After patch, both paths use the shared safe wrapper:

```ts
const safeFriendlyError = isRawAssistantErrorPassthrough({ friendlyError, rawError })
  ? undefined
  : friendlyError;
return (safeFriendlyError || GENERIC_ASSISTANT_ERROR_TEXT).trim();
```

- Regression tests:
  - `handleAgentEnd > suppresses raw assistant error messages in user-facing lifecycle events`
  - `buildEmbeddedRunPayloads > suppresses raw assistant error messages in user-facing reply payloads`
- Synthetic canary: `SECRET_CANARY_69737`
- Current HEAD tested: `3308ada56ade7326afdb5aa0ecc8926900cb354b`
- Proof log: `/tmp/openclaw-pr69737-proof.log`
- Validation log: `/tmp/openclaw-pr69737-validation.log`

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: terminal assistant errors with unclassified raw upstream `errorMessage` must not send that raw text through user-facing lifecycle or reply payload delivery text.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout at HEAD `3308ada56ade7326afdb5aa0ecc8926900cb354b` in `/home/rev/projects/openclaw-issue-69737-error-boundary`, running the actual `handleAgentEnd` lifecycle function and actual `buildEmbeddedRunPayloads` reply payload builder through `node --import tsx`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: direct Node/tsx proof imports `src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.lifecycle.ts` and `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/payloads.ts`, passes a terminal assistant payload with `stopReason: "error"`, `content: []`, and `errorMessage: "SECRET_CANARY_69737"`, then captures user-facing lifecycle event text and reply payload text.

- Evidence after fix: terminal output from the direct `node --import tsx` proof command:

```json
{
  "command": "node --import tsx direct handleAgentEnd plus buildEmbeddedRunPayloads canary proof",
  "head": "3308ada56ade7326afdb5aa0ecc8926900cb354b",
  "containsCanaryInUserFacingLifecycleText": false,
  "containsGenericFallbackInUserFacingLifecycleText": true,
  "containsCanaryInUserFacingReplyPayloadText": false,
  "containsGenericFallbackInUserFacingReplyPayloadText": true,
  "replyPayloads": [
    {
      "text": "LLM request failed.",
      "isError": true
    }
  ],
  "internalRawPreviewRetainedForMaintainers": true
}
```

- Observed result after fix: `SECRET_CANARY_69737` is absent from both user-facing lifecycle text and user-facing reply payload text; both use the generic fallback. Internal diagnostic preview retention still reports true for maintainers.

- What was not tested: no live Telegram/Slack transcript was captured from this shell.

## Testing

```bash
OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers.formatassistanterrortext.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/payloads.errors.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.lifecycle.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/errors.test.ts
```

Result:

```text
Test Files  4 passed (4)
Tests  156 passed (156)
```

```bash
git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD
node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 --config .oxfmtrc.jsonc src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/errors.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.lifecycle.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.lifecycle.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/payloads.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/payloads.errors.test.ts
```

Result:

```text
All matched files use the correct format.
```
